Heading.purpose: Renders a heading element (h1-h6) with optional icon, inline markdown support, and size/alignment controls.

props.name: text
props.description: Heading text content, supports inline markdown

props.name: iconName
props.description: Icon name to display alongside heading text

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of text prop

css_selectors..heading: outer element (h1-h6 tag), has margin-top: var(--spacing-lg)
css_selectors..heading-inner: inner span controlling font-size and alignment
css_selectors..heading-text: inner span wrapping the actual text content

gotchas: When size prop is set, it overrides the native heading level's font-size from _typography.css
Without a size prop, the heading size comes from base _typography.css styles for the Tag level, NOT from any component class
Returns nothing (empty render) if no text, no slot content, and no _component prop
Heading.visual_behavior: Renders as a block-level heading element with the specified level. Default is an h2 with no explicit size class (inherits from base typography). Has 1.5rem top margin. Supports inline markdown in text prop (bold, italic, links). Icon displays inline at 0.9em of heading font size.

Text.purpose: Renders block-level markdown content (paragraphs, lists, headings) from a text prop or slot, with alignment control.

props.name: text
props.description: Markdown content rendered as block-level HTML

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of text prop
gaps: NO size prop - cannot control font size directly (use SimpleText for sized text)
gotchas:
Uses md.render() (block-level) not md.renderInline() - produces

tags around content
Returns nothing if no text and no slot content and no _component
Text.visual_behavior: Renders markdown as block-level HTML (paragraphs, lists, etc.) inside a div. Default is left-aligned body text at 1rem. Has 1.5rem top margin. Good for rich text content with multiple paragraphs.

SimpleText.purpose: Renders a single paragraph of inline markdown text with size and alignment controls.
slots: Default: Alternative content instead of text prop

css_selectors..simple-text-content:
the

element with size and alignment classes

gotchas: Uses md.renderInline() - no block-level elements, just inline formatting

Newlines in text are converted to
tags
SimpleText.visual_behavior: Renders a single paragraph with controllable font size. Uses inline markdown (bold, italic, links but no block elements). Has 1.5rem top margin. Good for subtitles or descriptive text that needs size control.

Button.purpose: Renders a styled button or link with text, optional icon, variant styling, and size control.

props.name: hideText
props.description: Show icon only, hide text label

props.name: popovertarget
props.description: ID of popover element to toggle

variant.primary: bg: var(--color-bg-brand)=#000, color: var(--color-text-on-brand)=#fff, border: transparent
variant.secondary: bg: var(--color-bg-muted)=#d4d4d4, color: var(--color-text-strong)=#000, border: transparent
variant.tertiary: bg: transparent, color: var(--color-text-strong)=#000, border: var(--color-border-strong)=#2a2a2a
variant.ghost: bg: transparent, color: var(--color-text-strong)=#000, border: transparent
variant.text: bg: transparent, color: var(--color-brand)=#000, border: transparent, padding: 0, font-size: var(--font-size-lg)

slots: Default: Alternative content for button label instead of text prop

css_selectors..button-inner: the actual clickable element (a or button)

gotchas: Outer element is a <span>, NOT the clickable element - the inner .button-inner is the actual link/button
Default element is 'a' (anchor) not 'button' - must set element='button' for non-link usage
Returns nothing if no text, no slot, no icon, and no _component
Button.visual_behavior: Renders as an inline button with rounded corners (4px default). Primary variant has dark bg with white text. Has hover/focus states with transitions. Icon displays inline at same size as text with 0.15em gap. No top margin (inline element).

Image.purpose: Renders an optimized responsive image with aspect ratio control, object-position, and background mode.

css_selectors..image: outer wrapper, display: block, margin-top: var(--spacing-lg), overflow: hidden
css_selectors..image-inner: inner div controlling aspect ratio and position
css_selectors..image-inner.background: height: 100%, width: 100%, object-fit: cover on img

gotchas: When background=true, the .image element becomes position: absolute with inset: 0 and margin-top: 0
Local images (starting with /src/) are loaded via import.meta.glob and get Astro's image optimization (Picture component)
External images render as plain without optimization
Returns nothing if no valid source and no _component
Image.visual_behavior: Renders a responsive image that fills its container width. With aspect ratio set, the image is cropped with object-fit: cover. In background mode, it fills the parent container absolutely. Has 1.5rem top margin in normal mode. Rounded mode adds 16px border-radius.

Icon.purpose: Renders an SVG icon from the astro-icon library with size, color, and optional background.

props.name: name
props.description: Icon name from the icon set

gaps: NO margin-top - unlike most other core elements
gotchas: size='none' is a special value used when Icon is embedded inside other components (Heading, Button) to inherit their font-size
Social icons (name contains 'social/') automatically get fill: currentcolor class
Background color only applies when background=true AND a color is set
Icon.visual_behavior: Renders an inline SVG icon. Default size is lg (1.625rem). With background=true, shows a rounded rectangle behind the icon with the color's light shade. No top margin - typically used inside other components.

Spacer.purpose: Renders an empty vertical space of a specified height.
gaps: NO margin-top - spacer itself has no margin, just height
Spacer.visual_behavior: Renders an invisible block that takes up vertical space. aria-hidden=true so screen readers skip it. Default is 1rem tall.

Divider.purpose: Renders a horizontal rule with configurable vertical padding.

props.name: paddingVertical
props.description: Vertical padding around the rule: none|xs|sm|md|lg|xl|2xl|3xl

gaps: NO margin-top - divider has no outer margin, only internal padding
Divider.visual_behavior: Renders a subtle 2px horizontal line with configurable padding above and below. Default has 1rem padding on each side. Line color is very light (border-subtle).

List.purpose: Renders a list (ul/ol) of items with optional icons, direction, size, and alignment control.

listType.icon: list-style: none, padding: 0, uses Icon components

direction.horizontal: flex-flow: row wrap, gap: 1em (or 3em for bullet/numbered)

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of items array
dom_structure: ListItem (for each item in items array)

css_selectors..list > ul, .list > ol: the list element with flex layout

gotchas: listType='numbered' renders as <ol>, other types render as <ul>
listType='icon' removes all native list styling including padding-inline-start
List.visual_behavior: Renders a vertical or horizontal list of items. Default is a vertical icon list with 1rem font size and 0.5em gap between items. Has 1.5rem top margin.

ListItem.purpose: Renders a single list item with optional icon and inline markdown text.
slots: Default: Alternative content instead of text prop
ListItem.visual_behavior: Renders as a list item with optional icon inline before the text. Icon is 1em square, vertically aligned. Text wraps naturally.

Embed.purpose: Renders an HTML embed (iframe) with aspect ratio control.

props.name: html
props.description: Raw HTML to embed (usually an iframe)

slots: Default: Alternative HTML content instead of html prop
gaps: NO horizontal-strip aspect ratio option (unlike Image)
gotchas: Content is rendered with set:html - raw HTML injection, no sanitization
Embed.visual_behavior: Renders an embed container with aspect ratio constraint. Default is landscape (4:3). Iframes inside fill the full width and height.

Video.purpose: Renders a video player supporting YouTube (lite-youtube), Vimeo (lite-vimeo), or native HTML5 video.

props.name: id
props.description: Video ID for YouTube or Vimeo

props.name: source
props.description: Video URL for native HTML5 video

css_selectors..video: the video element, display: block, margin-top: var(--spacing-lg)

gotchas: Uses lite-youtube and lite-vimeo web components for lazy loading third-party embeds
Native video type defaults to mp4
Video.visual_behavior: Renders a video player. For YouTube/Vimeo uses lightweight facade components that only load the real player on click. For native video, renders an HTML5 video element with controls.

Testimonial.purpose: Renders a blockquote-style testimonial with author image, name, description, and quoted text.

alignX.center: align-items: center, justify-content: center, text-align: center

slots: Default: Alternative content for the quote text

css_selectors..author: author section with flex row layout

gotchas: Quote text automatically gets curly quotes (Unicode 201C/201D) prepended/appended via CSS ::before/::after
Author image is rendered as a 80x80 circle with border-radius: 9999px
Testimonial.visual_behavior: Renders a bordered card with quote text at 1.625rem, followed by an author section with circular avatar (80px), name, and description. Default is centered. Quote has smart curly quotes added via CSS.

Counter.purpose: Renders an animated number counter that counts up when scrolled into view.

props.name: number
props.description: Target number to count up to

gotchas: Animation starts from 20% of the target number and runs for 500ms
Uses IntersectionObserver with 0.6 threshold - only triggers when 60% visible
Animation runs once and is never replayed (data-has-run flag)
Counter.visual_behavior: Renders a large number that animates counting up when scrolled into view. Default is very large (3.75rem heading size). Includes optional prefix and suffix text. Has 1.5rem top margin.

DefinitionList.purpose: Renders a definition list (<dl>) as a two-column grid of term/definition pairs.
slots: Default: Alternative content instead of items array
DefinitionList.visual_behavior: Renders a two-column grid of term/definition pairs. Terms are in the left column (auto width), definitions in the right (1fr). Has 1.5rem top margin.

DefinitionListItem.purpose: Renders a single term/definition pair inside a DefinitionList using CSS subgrid.
slots: Default: Alternative content for the definition (dd)
title: Alternative content for the term (dt)
gotchas: Returns nothing if either title or text is missing (both required)
DefinitionListItem.visual_behavior: Renders a row in the definition list grid using CSS subgrid to align with the parent's column structure. Title is bold on the left, text on the right.

Pagination.purpose: Renders a page navigation component with numbered links, ellipsis, and optional prev/next arrows.

props.name: page
props.description: Astro pagination object with currentPage, lastPage, url properties

dom_structure: a.pagination-arrow.pagination-prev (if showArrows and not first page)
a/span (page numbers with .current for active page)
a.pagination-arrow.pagination-next (if showArrows and not last page)
gotchas: Returns null if page is undefined or lastPage <= 1
Max 5 visible page numbers at a time
Pagination.visual_behavior: Renders a horizontal strip of page number links with borders. Current page has brand-colored background. Shows ellipsis (...) when pages are truncated. Optional prev/next arrows on the ends.

Split.purpose: Renders a two-column layout with configurable width distribution, vertical alignment, gap, and mobile behavior.

props.name: firstColumnContentSections
props.description: Content blocks for the first column

props.name: secondColumnContentSections
props.description: Content blocks for the second column

props.name: reverseOrderOnMobile
props.description: Reverse order when stacked on mobile

props.name: fixedWidth
props.description: Fixed width in px for fixed-flexible mode

props.name: minSplitWidth
props.description: Container width below which columns stack to 1fr

slots: first: Content for the first column
second: Content for the second column

css_selectors..split-inner.reversed: marks reversed order for mobile behavior

gotchas: Uses container query (@container max-width: {minSplitWidth}px) NOT viewport media query for responsive stacking
When reverse=true, the distribution mode is also reversed (e.g., third-two-thirds becomes two-thirds-third)
reverseOrderOnMobile=true causes the reversed first pane to have order: 2 on mobile
Each pane has container-type: inline-size for nested container queries
First child in each pane gets margin-top: 0 !important
Split.visual_behavior: Renders a two-column grid layout. Default is 50/50 with 1.5rem gap, top-aligned. Stacks to single column at container width below 700px (not viewport width). Each column is a container query scope.

Grid.purpose: Renders a responsive grid of items with configurable min/max item width, gap, and layout mode.

props.name: maxItemWidth
props.description: Maximum item width in px (center layout only)

layout.center: display: flex, flex-wrap: wrap, justify-content: center, items flex between min/max widths
layout.start: display: grid, auto-fill columns with minmax(minItemWidth, 1fr), left-aligned

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of items array

css_selectors..grid-inner.layout-center: flexbox, centered, items have min/max width

gotchas: layout='center' uses flexbox with max-width constraint on items - items center in the container and wrap
layout='start' uses CSS grid with auto-fill - items fill left-to-right, last row may have empty space
min/max item widths are passed via CSS custom properties
Grid.visual_behavior: Renders a responsive grid of items. Default is centered flexbox layout with items between 200-320px wide and 1rem gap. Items wrap to new rows automatically. Has 1.5rem top margin.

GridItem.purpose: Renders a single cell in a Grid with nested content sections.

props.name: contentSections
props.description: Content blocks to render inside this grid cell

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of contentSections
gotchas: First child gets margin-top: 0 forced
Only-child gets height: 100% to fill the grid cell
GridItem.visual_behavior: A container for content within a grid. Fills available height and width. Each grid item is a container query scope.

Card.purpose: Renders a card container with optional background image/color, before/after content slots, padding, max-width, border, rounded corners, link wrapping, and color scheme.

props.name: beforeContentSections
props.description: Content before main content (e.g., image above text)

props.name: backgroundImage
props.description: Background image config {source, alt, positionVertical, positionHorizontal}

props.name: link
props.description: URL to make entire card clickable

slots: Default: Main content area
before: Content before the main content
after: Content after the main content

css_selectors..card: outer wrapper, display: block, margin-top: var(--spacing-lg)
css_selectors..card-inner: inner container, height: 100%, position: relative, overflow: hidden
css_selectors.a.card-inner:hover: transform: scale(1.05) on hover when card is a link

gotchas: When link is set, card-inner renders as <a> and gets hover scale transform
Background image and background color are separate - image is rendered as an Image component with background=true, color is a separate div
colorScheme sets data-theme attribute to switch between light/dark
First and last children in content areas get margin stripped
Card has position: relative with z-index layering: background < background-image < content
Card.visual_behavior: Renders a card container that fills its parent height. Can have a background image/color behind the content. With border and rounded, looks like a traditional card. Link mode makes the entire card clickable with a hover scale effect. Has 1.5rem top margin.

ButtonGroup.purpose: Renders a group of buttons in a row or column layout with alignment control.

alignX.start: row: justify-content: flex-start, column: align-items: flex-start
alignX.center: row: justify-content: center, column: align-items: center
alignX.end: row: justify-content: flex-end, column: align-items: flex-end

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of buttonSections
gotchas: Uses is:global style - classes are NOT scoped to the component
ButtonGroup.visual_behavior: Renders buttons in a horizontal row (default) or vertical column with 0.5rem gap. Buttons wrap to new lines in row mode. Has 1.5rem top margin.

Accordion.purpose: Renders a collapsible accordion with multiple items, optional single-open mode.

props.name: openFirst
props.description: Open the first item by default

props.name: singleOpen
props.description: Only allow one item open at a time (uses HTML name attribute)

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of items array
gotchas: singleOpen=true uses the HTML <details> name attribute to create exclusive accordion behavior (native browser feature)
Accordion.visual_behavior: Renders a vertical stack of collapsible sections. Each item shows a title with chevron icon. Has 1.5rem top margin.

AccordionItem.purpose: Renders a single collapsible accordion item using the native <details>/<summary> pattern.

props.name: title
props.description: Title text shown in the summary

slots: Default: Alternative content for the expanded area

css_selectors..accordion-item-title: summary element, flex row, bold text, clickable

gotchas: First child in detail area gets margin-top: 0 forced
AccordionItem.visual_behavior: Renders a <details> element with a clickable summary that shows a title and chevron. The chevron rotates 180 degrees when expanded. Opening animates with a grow-out animation. Has a subtle border on the bottom.

Carousel.purpose: Renders a carousel/slider powered by Embla Carousel with slides, navigation arrows, and dot indicators.

props.name: slidesToScroll
props.description: Number of slides to scroll at once

props.name: slideWidthPercent
props.description: Width of each slide as percentage of viewport

align.start: slides align to start of viewport
align.center: slides align to center of viewport
align.end: slides align to end of viewport

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of slides array

css_selectors..carousel-inner: position: relative, contains CSS vars for slide sizing
css_selectors..indicator: 8px dot, border-radius: full, scale(1.2) when selected

gotchas: Uses Embla Carousel library (initialized via client-side JS)
autoScroll mode automatically hides indicators and arrows
Arrow buttons get border-radius: 9999px forced via JS
Slide width is controlled by CSS vars, not direct sizing
Carousel.visual_behavior: Renders a horizontal slider with navigation. Default shows one full-width slide at a time with prev/next arrows and dot indicators below. Supports partial slides (slideWidthPercent < 100) for peek/gallery effects.

CarouselSlide.purpose: Renders a single slide within a Carousel.
slots: Default: Alternative content instead of contentSections

css_selectors..slide: flex-basis from CSS var, flex-shrink: 0, container-type: inline-size

CarouselSlide.visual_behavior: A container for slide content. Takes its width from the parent carousel's CSS variables.

ContentSelector.purpose: Renders a tabbed content selector with configurable navigation position (side or top).

props.name: items
props.description: Array of ContentSelectorItem objects (title, subtext, iconName, contentSections)

navigationPosition.start: Side navigation: CSS grid with 2 columns (minmax(10rem,20rem) + 1fr), tabs on left
navigationPosition.top: Top navigation: flex layout, tabs flow horizontally above content

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of items array
gotchas: Uses CSS :has() and :checked pseudo-classes for tab selection - no JS needed for visual state
If no tab is checked, first tab's content is shown via CSS fallback
JS is used only for ARIA attribute updates, not for visual switching
ContentSelector.visual_behavior: Renders a tabbed interface. Default is side navigation: tabs on the left (10-20rem wide) with content on the right. Top mode shows tabs horizontally above the content panel. Selected tab has a strong border indicator.

ContentSelectorPanel.purpose: Renders a single tab/panel pair within a ContentSelector.

props.name: iconName
props.description: Optional icon for the tab heading

props.name: checked
props.description: Whether this tab is initially selected

props.name: groupName
props.description: Radio group name for exclusive selection

slots: Default: Alternative content for the panel

css_selectors..content-selector-tab: clickable tab label with hover state
css_selectors..content-selector-panel: hidden by default, shown when input:checked

gotchas: Uses hidden radio inputs + CSS :checked for show/hide - progressive enhancement pattern
Tab heading is rendered as an h3 regardless of context
ContentSelectorPanel.visual_behavior: Each panel consists of a hidden radio input, a clickable tab label with heading and optional subtext, and a content panel that shows when the tab is selected. Panel appears with a fade-in animation.

BentoBox.purpose: Renders a bento-style grid layout with items that can span multiple columns and rows.
slots: Default: Alternative content instead of items array

css_selectors..bento-box: outer wrapper, container-type: inline-size, margin-top: var(--spacing-lg)

gotchas: At container width < 700px: 3 and 4 column layouts collapse to 2 columns, all items forced to span 1
At container width < 450px: all layouts collapse to 1 column
Uses grid-auto-flow: dense - items may reorder to fill gaps
BentoBox.visual_behavior: Renders a bento-style grid where items can span multiple rows/columns. Default is 3 columns with 200px minimum row height and 1rem gap. Items are densely packed. Collapses to fewer columns at smaller container widths.

BentoBoxItem.purpose: Renders a single cell in a BentoBox that can span multiple columns and rows.
slots: Default: Alternative content instead of contentSections
dom_structure: div.bento-box-item[style='grid-column: span N; grid-row: span N']
gotchas: colSpan/rowSpan are applied via inline style, not CSS classes
At small container widths, spans are forced to 1 via !important
BentoBoxItem.visual_behavior: A grid cell that can span multiple columns and rows in the BentoBox. Content fills the full cell height. Single child stretches to fill.

Modal.purpose: Renders a popover modal dialog with a trigger button, backdrop, and content area.

props.name: label
props.description: Modal label for accessibility and id generation

slots: Default: Alternative content for the modal body
gotchas: Uses the native HTML popover API (popover='auto')
Modal has entrance/exit animations via CSS transitions
Close button is sticky at the top of the modal
Backdrop uses var(--color-overlay) for the dark overlay
Modal.visual_behavior: Renders a trigger button that opens a centered modal dialog with a dark backdrop. Modal slides up with fade-in animation. Has a sticky close button in the top-right. Content scrolls within the 85vh max-height.

CustomSection.purpose: Renders a page-level section with optional background image/color, color scheme, padding, max-width, and rounded corners. This is the primary page-building block that wraps content blocks.

props.name: label
props.description: Section label for auto-generated id and CMS

props.name: contentSections
props.description: Content blocks to render inside the section

props.name: backgroundImage
props.description: Background image config {source, alt, positionVertical, positionHorizontal, priority}

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of contentSections

css_selectors..content: main content area, container-type: inline-size, margin-inline: auto, z-index: layer-1

gotchas: Default paddingVertical is '4xl' (5rem) - this is different from Card which has no default padding
Rounded uses --radius-xl (24px), larger than Card's --radius-md (12px)
Content area has container-type: inline-size for nested container queries
First and last children get margin stripped
Background layers stack: background color < background image < content (all use z-index)
CustomSection.visual_behavior: Renders a full-width page section with generous vertical padding (5rem default). Can have a background image or color behind the content. Content is horizontally centered with optional max-width constraint. This is the primary wrapper for page-level content blocks.

Form.purpose: Renders a form element with POST method and multipart encoding, containing form block components.

props.name: formBlocks
props.description: Array of form block components to render

slots: Default: Alternative content instead of formBlocks
gotchas: Always uses method=post and enctype=multipart/form-data
Form.visual_behavior: Renders a form container with full width and 1.5rem top margin. Contains form field components.

Input.purpose: Renders a labeled text input field with optional icon, validation, and various input types.

props.name: iconName
props.description: Icon to display in the input

css_selectors..input: flex column container, gap: xs, margin-bottom: md
css_selectors..field-shell: icon wrapper with border and focus styles

gotchas: When iconName is set, input is wrapped in a .field-shell span that provides the border instead of the input itself
Input.visual_behavior: Renders a labeled text input with optional icon. Label is bold, above the input. Required fields show a red asterisk. Has 0.25rem gap between label and input, 1rem bottom margin.

Textarea.purpose: Renders a labeled multi-line text area input.

css_selectors..textarea: flex column, gap: xs, margin-bottom: md, width: 100%

Textarea.visual_behavior: Renders a labeled textarea with minimum 100px height. Vertically resizable. Same spacing pattern as Input.

Select.purpose: Renders a labeled dropdown select field with options.
slots: Default: Alternative option elements when no options array

css_selectors..select: flex column, gap: xs, margin-bottom: md, width: 100%

Select.visual_behavior: Renders a labeled dropdown select. Same spacing as other form fields.

SelectOption.purpose: Renders a single <option> element for a Select component.
slots: Default: Additional content after label

Submit.purpose: Renders a form submit button, wrapping the Button component with type=submit.
slots: Default: Alternative button content
gotchas: Wraps the Button component - all Button props/styles apply
Submit.visual_behavior: Renders a submit button with primary variant and 1rem top margin. Delegates to Button component.

ChoiceGroup.purpose: Renders a group of radio buttons or checkboxes in a fieldset.

css_selectors..choice-group: fieldset, flex column, gap: sm, padding: md

gotchas: multiple=true renders checkboxes with name[] array syntax, false renders radio buttons
ChoiceGroup.visual_behavior: Renders a group of radio or checkbox options in a bordered fieldset. Default is vertical layout. Horizontal mode uses a responsive grid.

Hidden.visual_behavior: Invisible hidden input. No visual output.

FileUpload.purpose: Renders a labeled file upload input.

css_selectors..file-upload: flex column, gap: xs, margin-bottom: md, width: 100%

FileUpload.visual_behavior: Renders a labeled file input. Browser provides the native file picker UI.

Segments.purpose: Renders a segmented control (button-style radio/checkbox group) with optional icons.

props.name: options
props.description: Array of {value, label, icon, checked} objects

css_selectors..segments: fieldset, flex column, no border, no padding

gotchas: First and last segments get rounded corners only on the outer edges
Adjacent segments share borders (inline-end border removed except last)
Segments.visual_behavior: Renders a horizontal row of button-like segments. Selected segment gets a brand-colored border. Segments share edges creating a pill-like group. Can show icons, text, or both.

Date.purpose: Renders a labeled date picker input.

css_selectors..date: flex column, gap: xs, margin-bottom: md, width: 100%

Date.visual_behavior: Renders a labeled date input with browser-native date picker.

Slider.purpose: Renders a toggle switch (checkbox styled as a slider).
gotchas: The actual <input> is hidden (opacity: 0), the visual is the .slider-track pseudo-element
Slider.visual_behavior: Renders a toggle switch with a 48x26px track and 20px circular knob. Unchecked shows gray track, checked shows dark track with knob slid right. Label text appears to the right of the switch.
